source: CT6-6 requirements §3 (TC-1 … TC-3): optimize agents' internal communication ('talk like a caveman') without harming external output — the sixth and final in-repo component

scenarios: compress drops filler, preserves content + code, and measures savings
acceptance_criteria: compress: filler-drop + phrase-subs, preserves content/identifiers/numbers/line-structure/code verbatim + prepositions/copulas
skill states NEVER compress external output (non-negotiable)
distinguishes internal vs external + enumerates external surfaces (user answer / API payloads / commits / PRs / test output)
heavier ML package (LLMLingua-style) framed as a 3rd-party app-layer option over the stdlib floor, measured against compression_stats
47 skills with token-compression entry across README/CLAUDE/CODEBASE_MAP
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only engine + a skill + tests + docs).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — a deterministic in-process text transform; the engine + CLI are exercised by the pytest suite (incl. real subprocess).
testing_authorizations.both_layer_front_to_back: N/A — single-layer (Python engine + skill doctrine) change.
